svg.title: Format Comparison — SVG vs Markdown vs HTML. SVG wins 4.5/5.
svg.desc: Compact README scorecard comparing SVG, Markdown and HTML as visual output formats for AI agents across five axes: portability, agent-readable metadata, visual fidelity, token efficiency and zero dependencies. SVG scores 4.5/5, Markdown 3.0/5, HTML 2.5/5. Verdict cells are dual-coded — fill geometry (solid full, lower-half partial, hollow none) and hue (emerald, amber, rose) — so they read without color. A machine-readable hw.comparison/1 scorecard and a compressed hwz/1 envelope are embedded in metadata.

hw:reasoning.hw:intent: A README-scale scorecard: settle in one glance which visual output format best serves AI agents, sized to embed in a repo without dominating the page.
hw:reasoning.hw:approach: Reduce the 1010x790 ASCEND matrix to a 900x478 banner. Drop the radial gauges, large legend, per-axis vector glyphs and heavy grid; keep the tri-valence verdict cells. Each cell encodes magnitude twice (Bertin redundancy): fill geometry — solid (full) / lower-half (partial) / hollow ring (none) — and hue — emerald / amber / rose. The winner column is grouped as one figure by an azure hero band + brighter header underline (common-region + continuity). The single winner score is promoted into a header chip so the verdict reads before the table is scanned.
hw:reasoning.hw:tradeoffs: Did NOT keep the radial gauges or full legend that justify "SVG flexes markdown cannot reach"; at README scale they cost height the embed cannot afford, so the score row carries the totals instead. Did NOT collapse to a flat RAG dashboard: azure stays reserved for identity (chip, hero band, winner underline) so brand and verdict semantics never collide, and warm/none hue stays a minority of the area. Magnitude is encoded twice (fill + hue) — slightly busier per cell — bought back by surviving grayscale, color-blindness and forced-colors via geometry alone.
hw:reasoning.hw:spatial-notes: ViewBox 900x478, ~60% of the ASCEND height. Axis-label column from x=34 to x=372; three verdict columns on 164.7px pitch, centers 454, 619, 784. Cells 100x34, r6, centered on column x and row y. Row pitch 50px x5 (down from ~72). Four ink registers at header div y=92, column div y=130, score seam y=380, footer y=442. Winner hero band 160px wide behind the SVG column. Verdict magnitude is redundant across fill geometry and hue.

hw:lineage.hw:note: Same hw.comparison/1 data payload as the ASCEND matrix; this is a presentational reduction (compact README scale), not a data change. Trajectory pair for the spatial model: ASCEND (1010x790, gauges+legend) -> readme-compact (900x478, score row).
